title: narcissus bulbocodium subsp. vulgaris
intro: Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. vulgaris, also known as the Hoop Petticoat or Small-cup Daffodil, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, nodding flowers and strap-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its potential to act as a diuretic and to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. vulgaris include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
